item.title: Kubernetes Taint and Toleration
item.author: Author
item.description: You must know Taint and Toleration Usually it uses not to bind PODs to specific Node. For example, there’s a Node that has GPU resources then you want not to bind PODs that don’t need GPU resources. At this time, you can use taint and toleration. Taint taint taint is to apply to the Node. Commands Add taint command structure 1 kubectl taint nodes [node-name] [key]=[value]:[taint-effect] Name Example [node-name] node01 [key] app [value] myapp [taint-effect] NoSchedule / PreferNoSchedule / NoExecute Add taint
